name: time-picker
title: Time Picker
description: A filterable time-of-day picker built on ComboBox: generates times at a fixed interval (every 30 minutes by default) between a start and end time, displays them in 12- or 24-hour form via Intl while storing a 24-hour "HH:mm" value, and lets users type to filter ("2:30" or "14:30") or pick from the list. Inherits the combobox keyboard contract and Field wiring.
useWhen: Choosing a time of day from predictable slots (appointment times, office hours, pickup windows)
The set of acceptable times is a fixed cadence (every 15, 30, or 60 minutes) users should pick from rather than free-type
Type-ahead ('2:30') is faster than scanning a long list of times
avoidWhen: Capturing a duration or elapsed time rather than a clock time (use a number input with a unit)
A precise timestamp to the second, or a date-and-time together (use separate date and time fields)
Any minute is valid and the value is truly free-form (use a plain time input)

keyboard: Tab moves focus to the input; Shift+Tab moves away
Down Arrow opens the list and moves the highlight to the next time; Up Arrow moves to the previous time
Typing filters the times against both the visible label and the 24-hour value, keeping focus in the input
Enter selects the highlighted time and closes the list
Escape closes the list; pressing Escape again clears the input
Home / End jump to the first / last time in the list
